title: Hartogia glandulosa
taxonomy: Hartogia glandulosa is classified within the family Sapindaceae. This classification places it as a member of the Sapindales order and a subclass of Rosidae in the larger angiosperm clade. As a part of the Sapindaceae family, Hartogia glandulosa is closely related to other plants such as the raintree and the soapberry. The Sapindaceae family is a diverse group of flowering plants that includes trees, shrubs, and climbers. Further classification can be broken down into more specific ranks such as family, genus, and species, but Hartogia glandulosa is primarily recognized within the Sapindaceae family.
uses: Hartogia glandulosa is a plant species used in traditional medicine. The plant is known for its potential antimalarial properties and has been used to treat various health conditions. In traditional medicine, extracts from Hartogia glandulosa are used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant's extracts also have antimicrobial properties, which help in preventing infections. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of Hartogia glandulosa.
